answers: Start climbing at an indoor gym — they provide gear and have routes for all levels. Take a beginner class first.
Climbing gear basics: shoes (tight fit), harness, chalk bag, belay device, and helmet for outdoor climbing.
Indoor vs outdoor: indoor is safer, controlled, and great for learning. Outdoor adds real rock, weather, and route-finding challenges.
Climbing tips: use your legs (not just arms), keep hips close to wall, trust your feet, and rest when you can.
